A test script that verifies hexadecimal to decimal conversion in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that assigns the hexadecimal value `0x3e7` to the variable `a` and then uses an `assert` statement to verify that `a` is equal to `999`. The hexadecimal value `0x3e7` is equivalent to the decimal number `999`, so the assertion will pass if the code executes correctly. This script demonstrates a basic use of hexadecimal notation and assertion in JavaScript. The functionality is narrow, focusing on a single operation and its validation.
